Container.Name: vLLM ROCm
Container.Overview: OpenAI-compatible vLLM API server using the official ROCm image for AMD GPUs. This is an API/backend service, not a model-manager WebUI.
Container.Description:
vLLM ROCm runs the official vllm/vllm-openai-rocm container on Unraid for AMD GPU inference.
This template is intended for AMD ROCm-capable GPUs and exposes the OpenAI-compatible API on port 8000. It maps /dev/kfd and /dev/dri into the container and stores model/cache data under /root/.cache/huggingface.
Important: vLLM is an API server, not a full WebUI. The WebUI button opens the /v1/models API endpoint for a quick health check. It does not provide browser-based model browsing, model downloading, or runtime configuration. To chat in a browser or pick from served models, pair this container with Open WebUI or another OpenAI-compatible frontend.
Models are selected at container startup through the Model Arguments field. For Hugging Face models, use --model organization/model-name. For a model already stored on the Unraid server, mount it through Local Models and use --model /models/path-to-model.
Start with a small model such as Qwen/Qwen3-0.6B, then adjust Model Arguments for larger models.
Container.Requires: AMD ROCm-capable GPU with /dev/kfd and /dev/dri available on the Unraid host. Radeon Top or equivalent AMD GPU host support may be required. Consumer Radeon GPU compatibility can depend on the ROCm/vLLM image version and selected model.

Config.Name: API Port
Config.Description: OpenAI-compatible vLLM API port. The WebUI button opens /v1/models as a health check, not a full browser UI.

Config.Name: Model Cache
Config.Description: Stores Hugging Face model downloads and cache files.

Config.Name: Local Models
Config.Description: Optional folder for manually downloaded local models. Use --model /models/your-model-folder in Model Arguments.

Config.Name: Hugging Face Token
Config.Description: Optional Hugging Face token for gated or private models.

Config.Name: Model Arguments
Config.Description: Arguments passed to vLLM after the image name. Examples: --model Qwen/Qwen3-0.6B or --model /models/my-local-model --dtype auto --gpu-memory-utilization 0.90 --max-model-len 8192
